frozen_components: host model (frozen during relative representation computation)
architecture.summary: The paper proposes using relative representations (latent similarity between each sample and a fixed set of anchors) as an invariant data representation across neural networks. A sample's encoding in one latent space is mapped to similarities with anchor points, creating a representation that is invariant to latent isometries and rescalings. This enables zero-shot communication between latent spaces from different models or training runs without additional training. Information flows from a model's latent space through similarity computation with frozen anchors to produce the relative representation, which can then be used for downstream tasks or cross-model comparison.

biblio.title: Relative representations enable zero-shot latent space communication

claims.statement: Under the same data and modeling choices, the angles between the encodings within distinct latent spaces do not change
Relative representations enforce invariance to latent isometries and rescalings without any additional training
The approach enables zero-shot model stitching and latent space comparison between diverse settings

The generalization capability is validated across different datasets, spanning various modalities (images, text, graphs), tasks (e.g., classification, reconstruction) and architectures (e.g., CNNs, GCNs, transformers)
